---
title: "Simple SDM prediction"
author: "Robert Wildermuth"
date: "2025-9-24"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, loadLibraries}
library(mgcv)
library(tidyr)
library(lubridate)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
library(gbm)
library(dismo)
library(viridis)
library(sqldf)
source("../R/buildSDM.R")
source("../R/scoreSDM.R")
source("../R/testSkillSDM.R")
source("../R/runDiagnostics.R")
```

# Perform simple forecast predictions from an SDM model

Load the data for our previously fit SDM model
```{r, loadSDM}
# Load biological observation data with environmental covariates extracted
# This is now updated to include variables extracted at a lag of 1 year, which are used to calculate
# persistence skill (variables labeled with "_lag1")
# Is assumed that the columns "lon", "lat", and "date" are present and complete
obs <- readRDS("../data/combinedBioObs_envExtracted.rds")
# If observations don't have a "year", "month" and "quarter" columns, add them. Will need a "date" column to get them
if(!"year" %in% colnames(obs)) {
  obs$year <- year(obs$date)  
}
if(!"month" %in% colnames(obs)) {
  obs$month <- month(obs$date)  
}
if(!"quarter" %in% colnames(obs)) {
  obs$quarter <- quarter(obs$date)  
}

# I'm missing predictor variables for 2024, so I'm trimming that year off
obs <- subset(obs, year <= 2023)
# Define the target variable: here presence/absence of anchovy
obs$pa <- obs$anchPA

sdmType <- "brt" # Can currently be "gam" or "brt"
k <- 4 # Number of knots for a GAM. Will be ignored if not building a GAM
tc <- 3 # tree complexity for a BRT. Will be ignored if not building a BRT
lr <- 0.02 # learning rate for a BRT. Will be ignored if not building a BRT
max.trees <- 10000 # max trees for a BRT. Will be ignored if not building a BRT
varNames <- c("sst", "ild", "sst_sd", "ssh_sd", "logChl", "logEKE", "distLand", "anchssb") # Names of predictors in the SDM
targetName <- "pa" # Target variable for the SDM

#!!RW: do we need to load the full model data fit? What is there to compare among these outputs?
```

## Predict to previously observed locations
```{r}
# Predict at 1-year forecast horizon
horizon <- 1
  
# Call scoreSDM to build an SDM using subObs as training and test data
modH1 <- scoreSDM(subObs = obs, sdmType = sdmType, varNames = varNames,
                       targetName = targetName, k = k, tc = tc, lr = lr, max.trees = max.trees, 
                       yrsToForecast = horizon, includePersistence = TRUE)
# Call testSkillSDM to assess the skill of the X-year forecast for this SDM
aucCutoff <- 10 # If less observations than this cutoff within a month/season etc., AUC will not be calculated
sdmSkillH1 <- testSkillSDM(mod = modH1, targetName = targetName, aucCutoff = aucCutoff, usePersistence = FALSE)
# Add noYrs and sdmType to output
yrs <- unique(sort(obs$year))
sdmSkillH1$noYrsTrain <- length(yrs) - horizon
sdmSkillH1$terminalYr <- max(obs$year) - horizon 
sdmSkillH1$sdmType <- sdmType
  
# If you want to assess persistence skill, add a new list element showing sdmSkill for those predictions
sdmSkillPersH1 <- testSkillSDM(mod = modH1, targetName = targetName, aucCutoff = aucCutoff, usePersistence = TRUE)
# Add noYrs and sdmType to output
sdmSkillPersH1$noYrsTrain <- length(yrs) - horizon
sdmSkillPersH1$terminalYr <- max(obs$year) - horizon 
sdmSkillPersH1$sdmType <- sdmType
  
  
# Return some simple diagnostics
evalOutputs <- runDiagnostics(mod = modH1$sdm, max.trees = max.trees)
  
# plot the predictions and observations
  

```

What about a different forecast horizon?
```{r, horiz3}
# Predict at 3-year forecast horizon
horizon <- 3
  
# Call scoreSDM to build an SDM using subObs as training and test data
modH3 <- scoreSDM(subObs = obs, sdmType = sdmType, varNames = varNames,
                       targetName = targetName, k = k, tc = tc, lr = lr, max.trees = max.trees, 
                       yrsToForecast = horizon, includePersistence = TRUE)
# Call testSkillSDM to assess the skill of the X-year forecast for this SDM
aucCutoff <- 10 # If less observations than this cutoff within a month/season etc., AUC will not be calculated
sdmSkillH3 <- testSkillSDM(mod = modH3, targetName = targetName, aucCutoff = aucCutoff, usePersistence = FALSE)
# Add noYrs and sdmType to output
yrs <- unique(sort(obs$year))
sdmSkillH3$noYrsTrain <- length(yrs) - horizon
sdmSkillH3$terminalYr <- max(obs$year) - horizon 
sdmSkillH3$sdmType <- sdmType
  
# If you want to assess persistence skill, add a new list element showing sdmSkill for those predictions
sdmSkillPersH3 <- testSkillSDM(mod = modH3, targetName = targetName, aucCutoff = aucCutoff, usePersistence = TRUE)
# Add noYrs and sdmType to output
sdmSkillPersH3$noYrsTrain <- length(yrs) - horizon
sdmSkillPersH3$terminalYr <- max(obs$year) - horizon 
sdmSkillPersH3$sdmType <- sdmType
  
  
# Return some simple diagnostics
evalOutputs <- runDiagnostics(mod = modH3$sdm, max.trees = max.trees)

# plot the predictions and observations
```

## Predict to the full spatial surface
```{r}
# construct a mesh of evenly-spaced points within the sample frame
#!!RW: I think this is what Nerea did in 3_Predict_native.R
```

## Compare the skill for each prediction
```{r, testSkill}
sdmSkillAll <- rbind(sdmSkillH1, sdmSkillH3)

# Aggregate so skills are averaged across different months/seasons. "year" is the test year
sdmSkillAgg <- aggregate(auc ~ time + space + noYrsTrain + terminalYr + year, sdmSkillAll, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
# Add a field converting year to forecast time horizon
sdmSkillAgg$forecastHorizon <- sdmSkillAgg$year - sdmSkillAgg$terminalYr

# also compare to a persistence assumption
sdmSkillPers <- rbind(sdmSkillPersH1, sdmSkillPersH3)

# Aggregate so skills are averaged across different months/seasons. "year" is the test year
sdmSkillPersAgg <- aggregate(auc ~ time + space + noYrsTrain + terminalYr + year, sdmSkillPers, FUN = mean, na.rm = TRUE)
# Add a field converting year to forecast time horizon
sdmSkillPersAgg$forecastHorizon <- sdmSkillPersAgg$year - sdmSkillPersAgg$terminalYr

  
# spatial plot of difference in prediction skill and observations
```
